figures.caption: Fig. 1: Comparison of our proposed FaceLiVTv2 with other lightweight FR models, including EdgeFace [3], SwiftFaceFormer [4], and KANFace [5]. Mean accuracy of Face Benchmark v.s. Mobile Latency (iPhone 15 Pro). The upper-left region of the plot represents the most desirable operating point, where high accuracy is achieved with minimal latency.

TABLE 1: Key architectural properties of representative lightweight face recognition models
TABLE 7: Ablation On The Overall Impact Of Components On FaceLiVTv2-S Variant with Glint360K as a Training Dataset
Fig. 2: FaceLiVTv2 architecture with Lite MHLA and structural reparameterization. Stages 1 and 2 use RepMix and the last stage combines RepMix with Lite MHLA as spatial mixer.
Fig. 11: Layer distribution provided by XCode.
TABLE 5: Cross-Platform Latency Comparison of FaceLiVTv2 with SOTA
Fig. 6: Model in CoreML runtime breakdown. Operations highlighted in the redbox represent memory-bound operations, where memory access dominates execution time over computation.

Fig. 3: Architecture comparison of (a) LiteMHLA in FaceLiVTv2 and (b) MHLA in FaceLiVTv1, (c) FaceLiVTv2 Block and (d) FaceLiVT Block. C, H and W denotes as the number of channel, height and width of feature map, h denotes as the number of head on the MHLA.

TABLE 17: Performance gap between lightweight FaceLiVTv2 variants and large-scale SOTA FR models. Mean Acc denotes the mean accuracy over LFW, CFP-FP, and AgeDB-30. FLOPs and mobile latency are measured at 112×112 input on iPhone 15 Pro.
Fig. 10: Latency test of (1) FaceLiVTv2-XS, (2) FaceLiVTv2S, (3) FaceLiVTv2-M, (4). FaceLiVTv2-L with XCode using iPhone 15 pro. All model is cverted from pytorch framework to coremltols.
Fig. 8: Performance evolution for FaceLiVTv2-S across different number of head configuration on IJBB and IJBC Dataset
TABLE 6: Comparison of FaceLiVTv2 Variant with SOTA on TinyFace
Fig. 7: Comparison of our proposed FaceLiVTv2 with other lightweight FR models on mean accuracy of FR benchmark dataset v.s. FLOPs and mean accuracy v.s i5-12500 CPU Latency.

Fig. 4: Structural Reparameterization on RepMix Block. This visualization illustrate the process of merging DW3×3, DW1×1, residual connection, and BN into single DW3×3. The residual connection is substituted by the identity weight with the dimension similar like DW1×1 weight.
